title: Tarenna alleizettei
taxonomy: Tarenna alleizettei is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The classification of Tarenna alleizettei includes its placement within the genus Tarenna, which is a group of flowering plants primarily found in tropical regions. Tarenna alleizettei is a part of the Rubiaceae family, one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. The Rubiaceae family is also known as the madder family or coffee family, with a vast array of plants that include coffee, gardenias, and teak trees. Tarenna alleizettei is native to Madagascar, which adds to its unique characteristics within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Tarenna alleizettei is used in traditional medicine, primarily in the regions where it is native, such as Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential antimalarial properties. It is said to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Tarenna alleizettei for medicinal use. The plant's roots are often used in traditional remedies.
